## [15:05 - 17:44]

but really an example of the benefits of having a great high performance culture. So for instance, most people think that out follow up and outbound is all about processes and systems. I always get questions, how do I get my guys to follow up more? How do I get them to do more outbound, whatever. And look, why those do matter, what's far more important is cultivating an environment and a team culture that creates the intrinsic motivation for salespeople to want to do the things that you want them to do, such as massive amounts of follow up. So the intrinsic motivation is key here. Most people try to require that they do a certain amount of follow ups and touches a day and all this different stuff and stuff. That's the wrong way to think about it. Those requirements are extrinsic motivation, not intrinsic, and if you do that, you're never gonna get as good results. So how do you create the intrinsic motivation while there's two steps? You create a high performance culture where not hitting projections is unacceptable. So we talked about that's a culture based on results. Not hitting projections is unacceptable. Then we create a projection, or sorry, then we create projections that are impossible to hit without doing follow up and self-gen. If we can do those two things, then guess what? Naturally, they're gonna be intrinsically motivated to do follow up, outbound, and self-gen. So the projections should be high enough to where even if the salesperson executes the week perfectly, they have about a 60 to maybe 75% chance of hitting it. I learned that from the COO of IBM. That pressure is gonna stretch them to new levels of performance that they never even knew were capable. I can know and think back at my career when there was times when, I don't know if knowingly or unknowingly my sales manager was doing that to me, and man, that made me hit numbers that I never thought I could hit. And that pressure allowed me to innovate a lot of the systems, processes, and procedures and follow up stuff and outbound stuff that I teach now that I never even were to create it without that pressure. But the key is making sure that the pressure is intrinsic. It's for their own reasons, not extrinsic, which is for your reasons. If not, this is gonna fail.
